scope_rsc_nextjs.content:
Research scope for target 37.64.195.242 focusing on React Server Components (RSC) and Next.js ecosystem.

Targets/Products:
- Next.js (v13.x through v15.x)
- React 18.x and 19.x with RSC enabled
- react-server-dom-* packages (react-server-dom-webpack, react-server-dom-esm)
- Node.js runtimes commonly used with Next.js (Node 18–22 LTS/current)
- Vercel/Serverless Next.js deployments (where applicable)

Platforms/Tech stack assumptions:
- Linux-based Node.js hosting or Vercel edge/serverless
- Bundlers/transpilers used by Next.js (SWC), Webpack where applicable

Timeframe:
- Prioritize 2024-01-01 through 2026-05-20 (latest)

Authoritative sources to consult:
- NVD (nvd.nist.gov), MITRE CVE, GitHub Advisory Database
- Vendor advisories (Vercel/Next.js release notes, React/Meta release notes)
- Package registries and changelogs (npm, react-server-dom-* packages)
- Security mailing lists and reputable researchers/blogs

Goal:
- Identify RCE, injection, deserialization, path traversal, authz bypass, and supply-chain issues in RSC/Next.js and core dependencies. Record CVEs, CVSS, affected versions, references, and patches/mitigations.

vuln_nextjs_req_smuggling_2024_34350.content: Next.js HTTP Request Smuggling / response queue poisoning when using rewrites. Severity: HIGH. CVSS v3.1: 7.5 (CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N). Affected: routes using next.config.js rewrites in vulnerable versions. Fixed in Next.js 13.5.1 and newer. References: NVD (CVE-2024-34350), GHSA-77r5-gw3j-2mpf.

vuln_nextjs_ssrf_server_actions_2024_343.content: Next.js SSRF in Server Actions when Host header is manipulated and application self-hosted; relative redirect to path starting with '/'. Severity: HIGH. CVSS v3.1: 7.5 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N). Fixed in Next.js 14.1.1. References: NVD (CVE-2024-34351), GHSA-fr5h-rqp8-mj6g.

affected_versions.Next.js: < 14.1.1 with Server Actions and specific redirect pattern

vuln_nextjs_dos_2024_39693.content: Next.js Denial of Service (DoS) condition leading to server crash. Severity: HIGH. CVSS v3.1: 7.5 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H). Fixed in Next.js 13.5 and later.

vuln_nextjs_cache_poisoning_2024_46982.content: Next.js cache poisoning in pages router non-dynamic SSR routes. Severity: HIGH. CVSS v3.1: 7.5. Affected: Next.js 13.5.1–14.2.9 (pages router, SSR non-dynamic). Fixed in 13.5.7, 14.2.10, and later. References: NVD CVE-2024-46982, GHSA-gp8f-8m3g-qvj9.

vuln_nextjs_imgopt_dos_2024_47831.content: DoS in Next.js image optimization leading to excessive CPU consumption. Severity: HIGH (NVD), Medium (GHSA context). Affected: Next.js before 14.2.7 (when using default image optimizer). Not affected if images.unoptimized=true or custom loader. Fixed in 14.2.7. References: NVD CVE-2024-47831, GHSA-g77x-44xx-532m.

summary_nextjs_prioritized_findings_2024.content:
Prioritized Next.js vulnerability summary for target 37.64.195.242 (research scope: 2024–2026)

Priority 1 – High risk if preconditions met
- CVE-2024-34350 (HTTP request smuggling / response queue poisoning)
  Impact: Integrity (H), no auth needed. Exploitable when next.config.js uses rewrites on vulnerable versions.
  Fix: Upgrade to >= 13.5.1 (or any currently supported latest, e.g., 14.2.10+/15.x).
  Mitigations: Until patching, disable/limit rewrites behind strict proxy normalization; ensure upstream proxy terminates/normalizes ambiguous requests; WAF rules for smuggling patterns.
  Refs: GHSA-77r5-gw3j-2mpf, NVD.

- CVE-2024-34351 (SSRF in Server Actions)
  Impact: Confidentiality (H), no auth needed. Preconditions: self-hosted Next.js, uses Server Actions, and a Server Action redirects to a relative path beginning with '/'; Host header manipulation.
  Fix: Upgrade to >= 14.1.1.
  Mitigations: Enforce strict Host header validation at proxy/app; avoid using user-controllable redirects; prefer absolute internal URLs; network egress filtering.
  Refs: GHSA-fr5h-rqp8-mj6g, NVD.

Priority 2 – High availability impact / cache integrity
- CVE-2024-46982 (Cache poisoning in pages router non-dynamic SSR routes)
  Impact: Availability/Integrity via poisoned CDN/app cache; unauthenticated.
  Affected: Next.js 13.5.1–14.2.9, when using pages router + non-dynamic SSR (e.g., pages/dashboard.tsx).
  Fix: Upgrade to 13.5.7, 14.2.10, or later.
  Mitigations: Minimize caching of SSR pages; verify Vary/Cache-Control headers; consider migrating to app router or apply patches.
  Refs: GHSA-gp8f-8m3g-qvj9, NVD.

Priority 3 – DoS issues
- CVE-2024-39693 (Server crash DoS)
  Impact: Availability (H), unauthenticated.
  Fix: Upgrade to >= 13.5.
  Mitigations: Rate limiting and WAF anomaly detection until upgraded.
  Refs: GHSA-fq54-2j52-jc42, NVD.

- CVE-2024-47831 (Image Optimization DoS)
  Impact: Availability (H) via CPU exhaustion; unauthenticated.
  Affected: Next.js < 14.2.7 when using default image optimizer. Not affected if images.unoptimized=true, custom images.loader, or hosted on Vercel.
  Fix: Upgrade to >= 14.2.7.
  Mitigations: Temporarily set images.unoptimized=true or use a custom loader; place image routes behind aggressive rate limiting.
  Refs: GHSA-g77x-44xx-532m, NVD.

Recommended action plan
- Patch baseline: move to latest supported Next.js (>= 14.2.10 or current 15.x), ensuring 14.1.1+ and 14.2.7+ fixes are included; re-run SCA after upgrade.
- Config hardening: validate Host header at proxy; restrict rewrites; review Server Actions for redirects; tune cache headers on SSR non-dynamic routes; consider app router migration where feasible.
- Compensating controls: WAF rules for request smuggling; rate-limit image/_next and SSR routes; egress filtering to reduce SSRF blast radius.
- Verification: after patching, regression-test affected paths (rewrites, Server Actions redirects, pages SSR cache behavior, image optimizer).

open_ports_86_214_102_157.content: Top 1000 TCP port scan results for 86.214.102.157. Open ports found: 80 (http), 443 (https). Closed: 53, 113. 996 filtered.

profile_web_86_214_102_157.content: Service/version detection for 86.214.102.157. Evidence suggests Orange Livebox router management interface. HTTP on 80 returns empty reply; HTTPS on 443 presents Livebox Nautilus certificate. TLS 1.2/1.3 supported. Likely WAN-exposed router UI but HTTP responses blocked or restricted from external network.

cve_2024_57725.content: Arcadyan Livebox Fibra PRV3399B_B_LT: unauthenticated modification of GPON link via /firstconnection.cgi; also info disclosure via /cgi/cgi_authpage.js (slid_value). DoS by disconnecting internet.
